content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Project Exploration Summary

I've thoroughly explored the CryptOnly Front Core project. Here's the complete breakdown:

### 1. **Framework & Tech Stack**

**Framework:** Next.js 16 with App Router (SSR/SSG capable)
**Language:** TypeScript 5 (strict mode)
**UI Library:** React 19
**State Management:** Zustand 5
**Styling:** SCSS (Sass) + Tailwind CSS 4
**i18n:** i18next with react-i18next (English & Russian)
**Linting:** ESLint 9
**Formatting:** Prettier

**Key Dependencies:**
- `react`: 19.2.3
- `next`: 16.1.6
- `zustand`: 5.0.11
- `tailwindcss`: 4
- `sass`: 1.97.3
- `react-i18next`: 16.5.4

### 2. **Project Structure (Feature-Sliced Design)**

The project follows **Feature-Sliced Design (FSD)** architecture:

```
/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ app/                          # Next.js App Router (pages)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ globals.css              # Design tokens + Tailwind entry
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ layout.tsx               # Root layout (fonts, providers)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ page.tsx                 # Home route (/)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ error.tsx                # Global error boundary
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ not-found.tsx            # 404 page
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ sign-up/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ page.tsx
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ (dashboard)/
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ layout.tsx           # Auth guard layout
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ accounts/
в”‚           в””в”Ђв”Ђ page.tsx
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ entities/                     # Domain entities (models + stores)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ session/                 # User session store (Zustand)
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ store.ts
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ types.ts
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ index.ts
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ balance/                 # Balance data
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ invoice/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ withdrawal/
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ features/                     # User actions & interactions
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ auth/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ register.ts
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ sign-in.ts
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ verify-email.ts
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ logout.ts
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ index.ts
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ company-setup/
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ api.ts
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ index.ts
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ shared/                       # Shared infrastructure
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ api/                     # HTTP client wrapper
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ config/                  # Constants & env
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ i18n/                    # i18next setup
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ config.ts
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ I18nProvider.tsx
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ useAppTranslation.ts
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ locales/
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ en/
в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ ru/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ lib/                     # Utilities
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ types/                   # Generic types
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ui/                      # Reusable components
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Modal.tsx
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ BackButton.tsx
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ CryptonlyLogo.tsx
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ LanguageSwitcher.tsx
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ HtmlLangSync.tsx
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ views/                        # Page-level compositions
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ sign-up/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ui/
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SignUpFlowClient.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SignUpFlow.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SignUpFirstStepPage.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SignUpVerifyEmailPage.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SignUpCompanyPage.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ *.scss
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ accounts/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ui/
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AccountsPage.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AccountsHeader.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AccountTable.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ TransactionHistory.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ WithdrawalModal.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ InvoiceModal.tsx
в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ *.scss
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ sign-in/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ ui/
в”‚
в””в”Ђв”Ђ widgets/                      # Composite UI blocks
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ app-layout/
    в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ui/
    в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ AppLayout.tsx
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ app-sidebar/
    в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ui/
    в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ AppSidebar.tsx
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ mobile-nav/
        в””в”Ђв”Ђ ui/
            в”њв”Ђв”Ђ MobileHeader.tsx
            в””в”Ђв”Ђ MobileBottomNav.tsx
```

### 3. **Key Pages & Routes**

| Route                    | Component                  | Type          | Auth Required |
|--------------------------|----------------------------|---------------|---------------|
| `/`                      | SignInPage                 | Public        | No            |
| `/sign-up`               | SignUpFlowClient           | Public        | No            |
| `/(dashboard)/accounts`  | AccountsPage               | Protected     | Yes           |

**Layout Pattern:**
- Root layout includes I18nProvider and global fonts
- Dashboard layout has authentication guard + AppLayout wrapper
- AppLayout includes AppSidebar, MobileHeader, MobileNav, and content area

### 4. **CSS & Styling Approach**

**Hybrid approach:**
- **Tailwind CSS 4** via PostCSS plugin for utility classes
- **SCSS** for component-specific styling using **BEM naming convention**
- **CSS Custom Properties** (CSS variables) for design tokens in `globals.css`

**Design Tokens** (defined in `/src/app/globals.css`):
```css
:root {
  --color-bg-primary: #111;
  --color-bg-surface: #191919;
  --color-text-primary: #ffffff;
  --color-accent: #648add;
  --radius-md: 10px;
  --sidebar-width: 265px;
  /* ... more tokens ... */
}
```

**SCSS Structure Example:**
```scss
.accounts-header {
  display: flex;
  
  &__title {
    font-size: 26px;
  }
  
  &__tab {
    &--active { }
  }
}
```

**File Organization:**
- Components: PascalCase (e.g., `AccountsPage.tsx`)
- Styles: kebab-case (e.g., `accounts-header.scss`)
- Styles co-located with components

### 5. **User-Related Components & Patterns**

**Existing User Components:**
- `AppSidebar.tsx` - Shows user info in sidebar, has user icons
- Navigation with role-based icons (Dashboard, Accounts, Members, Partnership, Settings)
- Session store tracks `user`, `accessToken`, `emailVerified`, `tenantId`

**Auth Protection Pattern:**
```typescript
// In (dashboard)/layout.tsx
const authenticated = !!accessToken && !!user && !!user.emailVerified && user.tenantId !== null;
useEffect(() => {
  if (!authenticated) router.replace('/');
}, [authenticated, router]);
```

**Modal Pattern** (for creating entities):
```typescript
// Modal.tsx + specialized sub-components
<Modal onClose={closeModal}>
  <ModalTitle>Title</ModalTitle>
  <ModalLabel>Label with input</ModalLabel>
  <ModalError>Error message</ModalError>
  <ModalActions>
    <ModalButton variant="secondary">Cancel</ModalButton>
    <ModalButton>Submit</ModalButton>
  </ModalActions>
</Modal>
```

**Data Fetching Pattern:**
```typescript
// AccountsPage.tsx example
const [data, setData] = useState<BalanceSummaryDto | null>(null);
const loadData = useCallback(async () => {
  try {
    const res = await fetchBalanceSummary();
    setData(res);
    return res;
  } catch { return null; }
}, []);

useEffect(() => {
  let cancelled = false;
  loadData().then(/*...*/);
  return () => { cancelled = true; };
}, []);
```

### 6. **Translation System**

**i18n Setup:**
- Configured in `src/shared/i18n/config.ts`
- Supports: English (en), Russian (ru)
- Locales in `src/shared/i18n/locales/[en|ru]/[namespace].json`
- Custom hook: `useAppTranslation(namespace)` - fully typed translations
- `HtmlLangSync.tsx` syncs `<html lang>` attribute with i18n language
- `LanguageSwitcher.tsx` component in shared UI

### 7. **Component Patterns Observed**

**Page-level Components:**
- Use `'use client'` directive for client-side rendering
- Often wrapped in dynamic imports with `ssr: false` (SignInPage)
- Delegate to view components

**View-level Components:**
- Contain page logic, state management, API calls
- Use Zustand for shared state (session, currency preference)
- Handle modals, tabs, and multi-step flows
- Example: `AccountsPage.tsx` manages accounts, balances, modals, tabs

**Reusable Components:**
- Modal system with composable parts
- Table components with grouping logic (USDT accordion)
- Header components with tabs and dropdowns
- Navigation components (sidebar, mobile nav)

### 8. **Key TypeScript Patterns**

- Path alias: `@/*` в†’ `src/*`
- Strict mode enabled
- Types imported with `type` keyword
- DTOs for API responses: `BalanceSummaryDto`, `TenantAccountBalanceSummaryDto`
- Zustand store patterns with type safety
- Component prop interfaces clearly defined

### 9. **AppSidebar Navigation**

**Menu Items:**
- Dashboard (icon)
- Accounts (icon)
- Members (icon) 
- Partnership (icon)
- Settings (icon)
- Exit/Logout (icon)

**Sidebar Features:**
- Grand total balance display
- Currency switching
- Language switcher
- User logout functionality
- Responsive (desktop sidebar + mobile nav)

### 10. **Mobile-First Responsive Design**

**Breakpoint:** 1023px (desktop/mobile split)
- Desktop: Full sidebar + desktop header + desktop tables
- Mobile: Hidden desktop elements, mobile header (top), mobile nav (bottom), FAB button

**Classes use `@media (max-width: 1023px)` for responsive hiding/showing**

---

## Key File Locations:

- **Root Layout:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/app/layout.tsx`
- **Global Styles:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/app/globals.css`
- **Dashboard Layout:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx`
- **AppLayout Widget:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/widgets/app-layout/ui/AppLayout.tsx`
- **AppSidebar:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/widgets/app-sidebar/ui/AppSidebar.tsx`
- **Accounts Page:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/views/accounts/ui/AccountsPage.tsx`
- **Shared UI Components:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/shared/ui/`
- **Package.json:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/package.json`
- **README:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/README.md`

1. The overall project structure (Next.js? React? What framework?)
2. Where pages are located
3. Where components are located
4. What CSS approach is used (CSS modules, Tailwind, styled-components, etc.)
5. Look at existing pages to understand the routing and layout patterns
6. Check for any existing user-related components
7. Check package.json for dependencies
8. Look at the layout files

Focus on the src directory and any pages/components folders. Show me the file tree and content of key files like package.json, any layout files, and existing page examples.

- Zustand store patterns with type safety
- Component prop interfaces clearly defined

### 9. **AppSidebar Navigation**

**Menu Items:**
- Dashboard (icon)
- Accounts (icon)
- Members (icon) 
- Partnership (icon)
- Settings (icon)
- Exit/Logout (icon)

**Sidebar Features:**
- Grand total balance display
- Currency switching
- Language switcher
- User logout functionality
- Responsive (desktop sidebar + mobile nav)

### 10. **Mobile-First Responsive Design**

**Breakpoint:** 1023px (desktop/mobile split)
- Desktop: Full sidebar + desktop header + desktop tables
- Mobile: Hidden desktop elements, mobile header (top), mobile nav (bottom), FAB button

**Classes use `@media (max-width: 1023px)` for responsive hiding/showing**

---

## Key File Locations:

- **Root Layout:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/app/layout.tsx`
- **Global Styles:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/app/globals.css`

- **AppSidebar:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/widgets/app-sidebar/ui/AppSidebar.tsx`
- **Accounts Page:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/views/accounts/ui/AccountsPage.tsx`
- **Shared UI Components:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/shared/ui/`
- **Package.json:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/package.json`
- **README:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/README.md`

message.content:
Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
   - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
   - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
7. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
8. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
9. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>
<analysis>
[Your thought process, ensuring all points are covered thoroughly and accurately]
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   [Detailed description]

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - [Concept 1]
   - [Concept 2]
   - [...]

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - [File Name 1]
      - [Summary of why this file is important]
      - [Summary of the changes made to this file, if any]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [File Name 2]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [...]

4. Errors and fixes:
    - [Detailed description of error 1]:
      - [How you fixed the error]
      - [User feedback on the error if any]
    - [...]

5. Problem Solving:
   [Description of solved problems and ongoing troubleshooting]

6. All user messages: 
    - [Detailed non tool use user message]
    - [...]

7. Pending Tasks:
   - [Task 1]
   - [Task 2]
   - [...]

8. Current Work:
   [Precise description of current work]

9. Optional Next Step:
   [Optional Next step to take]

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:
<example>
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing the conversation focus on typescript code changes and also remember the mistakes you made and how you fixed them.
</example>

<example>
# Summary instructions
When you are using compact - please focus on test output and code changes. Include file reads verbatim.
</example>


IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any tools. You MUST respond with ONLY the <summary>...</summary> block as your text output.

content.text:
<analysis>
Let me analyze the conversation carefully:

1. The user has a task to implement a "Users" (tenant users) page based on CSS specifications from a Figma design export.

2. The user provided an extremely long CSS specification that describes:
   - A Users list page with a table showing: User (name + avatar), Email, Role (badge), Status (badge), Last activity, Actions
   - A sidebar navigation with menu items (Dashboard, Accounts, Members/Users, Partnership, Settings, Logout)
   - An "Add User" slide-out panel with email input and role dropdown
   - Access rights by roles section with two cards: Administrator (orange gradient) and Employee (purple gradient)
   - Mobile versions of the same screens
   - Various states: default, dropdown open, form validation error, invitation sent confirmation

3. An Explore agent was launched to understand the project structure, which returned detailed information about:
   - Next.js 16 with App Router
   - TypeScript 5
   - Feature-Sliced Design (FSD) architecture
   - SCSS + Tailwind CSS 4 for styling
   - Zustand for state management
   - i18next for internationalization
   - The existing pages/components structure

4. No code was actually written yet - the exploration just completed and was about to start implementing.

Key design elements from the CSS:
- Background: #141414 (dark) for sidebar, #F4F4F4 for main content area
- Sidebar: 265px wide, dark background
- Main content: white cards with rounded corners
- Table columns: User (240px), Email (250px), Role (100px), Status (100px), Last activity (140px), Actions (305px)
- User avatar: 40px circle with teal (#31ECE6) background showing initials
- Role badges: Administrator = orange (#F9C78E on rgba(249,203,142,0.2)), Employee = purple (#E98EF9 on #FBE8FE)
- Status badges: Active = green (#10B981 on #E5F7F1), Inactive = gray (#8E8E8E on rgba(213,213,213,0.2))
- "Add User" button: blue (#8EA9F9) with + icon
- Add User panel: slides in from right (481px wide)
- Access rights section: Two gradient cards side by side
- Font: 'Involve' throughout

The pending task is to implement the Users page with:
1. A route at `/(dashboard)/users` (following existing pattern)
2. A page component in `src/views/users/ui/`
3. Sidebar navigation item for Users (already exists as "Members")
4. The table with user data
5. The "Add User" slide-out panel
6. Access rights section

No files were created or modified yet - the exploration just completed.
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wants to implement a "Users" (tenant users) page based on a Figma design export provided as raw CSS specifications. The design shows:
   - A main Users page with a data table (columns: User with avatar+name, Email, Role badge, Status badge, Last activity, Actions with 3-dot menu)
   - A dark sidebar (265px) with navigation items: Dashboard, Accounts, Users/Members, Partnership, Settings
   - An "Add User" slide-out panel (481px wide) appearing from the right with email input, role dropdown, Cancel and Save buttons
   - An "Access rights by roles" section with two gradient cards: Administrator (orange gradient `linear-gradient(88deg, #EDB26F -7.81%, #F9C78E 98.32%)`) and Employee (purple gradient `linear-gradient(87.48deg, #DB78EC 1.32%, #E98EF9 97.89%)`)
   - Mobile-responsive versions with bottom tab bar and sheet modal for Add User
   - Form validation state (red border on email input with error message)
   - Invitation sent confirmation state (success screen)
   - Role badges: Administrator = `rgba(249,203,142,0.2)` background with `#F9C78E` text, Employee = `#FBE8FE` background with `#E98EF9` text
   - Status badges: Active = `#E5F7F1` bg / `#10B981` text, Inactive = `rgba(213,213,213,0.2)` bg / `#8E8E8E` text

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js 16 with App Router (route groups, layouts)
   - Feature-Sliced Design (FSD) architecture: `app/`, `entities/`, `features/`, `shared/`, `views/`, `widgets/`
   - TypeScript 5 with strict mode
   - React 19
   - SCSS with BEM naming conventions, co-located with components
   - Tailwind CSS 4 for utility classes
   - Zustand 5 for state management (session store)
   - i18next / react-i18next for EN/RU internationalization
   - CSS Custom Properties (design tokens) in `globals.css`
   - Route protection via `(dashboard)/layout.tsx` auth guard
   - Font family: 'Involve' used throughout the design

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/app/(dashboard)/accounts/page.tsx`
      - Existing protected page as reference for route pattern
   - `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx`
      - Auth guard layout that wraps all dashboard routes; new users route needs to be placed under this group
   - `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/views/accounts/ui/AccountsPage.tsx`
      - Reference implementation for page-level component patterns (state, API calls, modals)
   - `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/widgets/app-sidebar/ui/AppSidebar.tsx`
      - Sidebar with navigation; may need updating to add/highlight "Users/Members" nav item
   - `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/app/globals.css`
      - Design tokens as CSS custom properties
   - `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/crypto/front-core/src/shared/ui/Modal.tsx`
      - Existing modal pattern with composable parts for reference

   Key design values extracted from CSS:
   - Sidebar width: 265px, bg: `#141414`
   - Content area bg: `#F4F4F4`, border-radius: `32px 0px 0px 32px`
   - Table card: white bg, `border-radius: 16px`, padding: `20px`, gap: `23px`
   - Avatar: 40px circle, bg: `#31ECE6`, white initials
   - Add User button: `#8EA9F9`, border-radius: `12px`, `padding: 12px 18px 12px 12px`
   - Add User panel: 481px wide, white bg, `border-radius: 32px 0px 0px 32px`
   - Input/dropdown: border `1px solid #E1E1E1`, border-radius: `12px`, height: `48px`
   - Error state input: border `1px solid #E64B4B`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - No errors encountered yet; exploration phase just completed.

5. Problem Solving:
   - The Explore agent successfully mapped the entire project architecture to inform implementation decisions.
   - Key insight: New users page should follow the pattern of `src/views/accounts/` with a corresponding route at `src/app/(dashboard)/users/page.tsx`.
   - The "Members" nav item likely already exists in the sidebar and just needs the correct route linkage.

6. All user messages:
   - The user sent a very long message containing: a task description ("Сѓ РјРµРЅСЏ СЃС‚РѕРёС‚ Р·Р°РґР°С‡Р° Р РµР°Р»РёР·РѕРІР°С‚СЊ СЃС‚СЂР°РЅРёС†Сѓ СЃ РїРѕР»СЊР·РѕРІР°С‚РµР»СЏРјРё С‚РµРЅР°РЅС‚Р° РІРѕС‚ css") followed by an extremely large raw CSS export from Figma describing the complete Users page design across multiple screen states (desktop list view, add user panel, mobile views, error states, success states). Also included was a screenshot image showing the overall Figma canvas layout with multiple screen states.

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Create `src/app/(dashboard)/users/page.tsx` (route entry point)
   - Create `src/views/users/ui/UsersPage.tsx` (main page component)
   - Create `src/views/users/ui/UsersHeader.tsx` (page header with "Users" title and "Add User" button)
   - Create `src/views/users/ui/UsersTable.tsx` (users data table)
   - Create `src/views/users/ui/AddUserPanel.tsx` (slide-out panel for adding users)
   - Create `src/views/users/ui/AccessRightsSection.tsx` (role cards section)
   - Create corresponding SCSS files for all components
   - Update sidebar to link "Users/Members" nav item to `/users` route
   - Add i18n translation keys for EN and RU

8. Current Work:
   The Explore agent just finished mapping the project architecture. No code has been written yet. The exploration revealed the project uses FSD architecture with SCSS+BEM styling, and the new Users page should follow the same patterns as the existing Accounts page. The task is at the very beginning of the implementation phase.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Begin implementing the Users page by creating the necessary files following the FSD pattern. The user's request was: "Сѓ РјРµРЅСЏ СЃС‚РѕРёС‚ Р·Р°РґР°С‡Р° Р РµР°Р»РёР·РѕРІР°С‚СЊ СЃС‚СЂР°РЅРёС†Сѓ СЃ РїРѕР»СЊР·РѕРІР°С‚РµР»СЏРјРё С‚РµРЅР°РЅС‚Р° РІРѕС‚ css" (implement the tenant users page based on the provided CSS).

   Start with:
   1. `src/app/(dashboard)/users/page.tsx` - route entry
   2. `src/views/users/ui/UsersPage.tsx` - main component with table, header, and add user panel
   3. `src/views/users/ui/users-page.scss` - styles following SCSS/BEM conventions matching the Figma design
</summary>
